Metadata.title: Nemanzh Software Studio | Enterprise .NET and Full-Stack Development
Metadata.description: Nemanzh Software Studio is my independent software practice focused on enterprise applications, scalable .NET backends, cloud solutions, and modern web platforms.

hero.availability: Open to selected enterprise projects and long-term technical partnerships.
hero.title: .NET-led enterprise software for teams that need stable delivery and clean systems.
hero.lead: I build enterprise applications, scalable .NET backends, and cloud-connected web platforms for teams that need dependable execution.
hero.body: Nemanzh Software Studio is how I present my work: senior delivery across insurance software, public sector systems, internal business platforms, and modern web products, with a focus on maintainable systems and direct communication.

stats.label: years across enterprise and product systems

about.title: An independent software studio built around senior enterprise delivery.
about.intro: I am not trying to look larger than I am. The value is direct senior execution, strong backend engineering, and systems that stay understandable after release.
paragraphs: I build enterprise applications and internal business platforms for teams that need solid architecture, reliable delivery, and backend engineering that does not collapse under day-to-day business use.

My background spans Nordic insurance software, public sector systems, identity and integration-heavy enterprise environments, and payment-enabled web products. That range helps me move between product work, operational constraints, and long-term maintenance without unnecessary complexity.

I am strongest when .NET is at the core and the rest of the stack needs to work with it cleanly: Next.js or Angular frontends, SQL-heavy systems, cloud services, integrations, upgrades, and production ownership.

highlights.label: Focus
highlights.value: Enterprise applications, backend engineering, cloud solutions

highlights.label: Approach
highlights.value: Clear ownership, measured delivery, maintainable systems

highlights.label: Fit
highlights.value: Insurance, SaaS, internal platforms, modernization work

services.title: Enterprise application development with .NET in the foreground.
services.intro: My services center on fullstack development, scalable systems, cloud solutions, and backend engineering that support real business operations.

items.title: Enterprise Application Development
items.description: Business-critical web applications built around clear architecture, dependable delivery, and maintainable code paths.

items.title: .NET Backend Engineering
items.description: C#, ASP.NET, APIs, and core business logic designed for reliability, change, and steady system growth.

items.title: Cloud and Multi-Tenant Solutions
items.description: Cloud-ready architecture, tenant-aware systems, caching, secrets management, and deployment workflows across Azure and AWS.

items.title: System Integrations and Identity
items.description: Payments, federated login, identity lifecycle automation, and message-driven synchronization between business systems.

items.title: Modernization and Upgrades
items.description: Incremental upgrades across .NET, Angular, databases, and legacy enterprise systems without risky rewrites.

items.title: Internal Tools and Automation
items.description: Operational software, reporting flows, and automation that reduce repetitive work and improve visibility.

experience.title: Experience presented as actual roles, with enterprise delivery in the foreground.
experience.intro: The pattern across these roles is consistent: backend responsibility, modernization work, integrations, and business software that has to stay stable while it evolves.

items.summary: Working on enterprise modernization projects focused on Single Sign-On across multiple applications, backend services, frontend applications, and cloud-based environments.
items.impact: I am implementing SSO across multiple enterprise applications, supporting .NET, Angular, React, TypeScript, SQL Server, Redis, RabbitMQ, and Azure migration work inside a 50-person Kanban delivery team.

items.summary: Worked on governance, membership, payment, and blockchain-related products within the Cardano ecosystem.
items.impact: I built Next.js and Strapi-based application flows, integrated Stripe and crypto wallet payments, implemented Web3Auth, generated and encrypted Cardano keys, added AWS SQS communication, and supported Docker Compose delivery on AWS and Qovery.

items.summary: Worked on a large in-house insurance platform used by Nordic insurance companies across backend, frontend, database, cloud, and DevOps areas.
items.impact: I developed insurance software with C#/.NET, Angular, SQL Server, and T-SQL, improved core business modules, helped modernize the stack toward .NET 8 and Angular 16, optimized stored procedures, and supported Azure Redis Cache, Azure Key Vault, and deployment workflows.

items.role: Full Stack Developer, Public Cloud Sector
items.summary: Worked on Microsoft-based enterprise and public sector solutions, including identity management, SharePoint modernization, cloud migration, automation, and application maintenance.
items.impact: I implemented Microsoft Identity Manager across Azure Active Directory and on-prem Active Directory, maintained the WPF-based One Stop Shop application for the Central Registry of Compulsory Social Insurance, supported SharePoint Online migration, and extended workflows with React, Power BI, and Power Automate.

items.summary: Started as a backend-focused .NET developer working on REST APIs, database-driven systems, public sector applications, and internal development tooling.
items.impact: I developed ASP.NET services for public-sector clients, improved authorization workflows, built code generators to reduce repetitive work, and created SQL Server tables, stored procedures, and schema updates for business-critical systems.

projects.title: Selected platforms and client projects.
projects.intro: A few recognizable systems and organizations I have worked on, shown as straightforward project cards.

labels.title: How I Delivered
labels.intro: A short breakdown of the problem, my role, and the engineering approach behind this work.

items.title: National Employment Service
items.description: Backend work for a public-sector platform with REST APIs, authorization logic, and SQL Server-backed business workflows.
items.impact: The focus was reliability, structured domain logic, and maintainable delivery inside an in-house ASP.NET framework.

items.title: Dual Education
items.description: Backend support for the Serbian dual education platform, covering API logic, data workflows, and public-sector delivery constraints.
items.impact: The work centered on stable backend behavior, clear data handling, and software that could support administrative processes without unnecessary complexity.

items.title: Unionen
items.description: Enterprise platform work delivered through Insicon for the Swedish market, with .NET, Angular, SQL Server, and long-lived business workflows.
items.impact: The emphasis was on maintainability, feature delivery, and modernization inside a production system rather than one-off implementation work.

caseStudy.problem: Business and insurance-related applications needed stable functionality, better performance, and maintainable feature development across different team sizes.
caseStudy.role: I worked as a full-stack developer on .NET, Angular, and SQL Server systems, contributing to feature development, performance improvements, and maintenance.
caseStudy.solution: Implemented and improved application features, optimized data access where needed, improved frontend behavior, and worked with teams in Agile/Scrum delivery processes.
caseStudy.impact: Helped improve system reliability, user workflows, and maintainability in business-facing applications.

items.title: ICA Forsakring
items.description: Insurance platform work in the Swedish market, spanning enterprise application delivery, backend changes, frontend work, and cloud-oriented modernization.
items.impact: I contributed across the stack in a production insurance environment where continuity, upgrades, and long-term maintainability mattered more than fast cosmetic change.

items.title: Deluxe Corporation
items.description: Work connected to a US-based payments and data technology company that supports businesses, financial institutions, and enterprise clients with digital financial solutions.
items.impact: The focus was on systems that help organizations manage payments, process transactions, and use data-driven services to improve operations and support growth.

caseStudy.problem: The application needed a secure enterprise SSO flow that worked consistently across frontend, backend, and multiple environments such as QA and UAT.
caseStudy.role: I worked on the frontend authentication flow, MSAL setup, environment-specific configuration, and coordination with backend/API and deployment teams. The work was done through Quadro Consulting for Deluxe.
caseStudy.solution: Implemented the frontend Azure AD authentication flow, connected it with backend token exchange through IntegrationService API, handled configuration for different environments, and supported QA/UAT validation.
caseStudy.impact: Helped deliver a clearer and more reliable authentication flow for an enterprise application, with better alignment between frontend, backend, and deployment process.

items.title: IntersectMBO
items.description: Work on a blockchain-oriented platform focused on product logic, backend services, and stable execution of key user and transaction flows.
items.impact: The emphasis was on transaction reliability, clearer product behavior, and a system that could support growing demand without losing operational stability.

caseStudy.problem: The platform needed reliable member-facing features, voting workflows, admin functionality, and integrations with external systems.
caseStudy.role: I worked across frontend and backend features, helped build and improve user flows, connected APIs, and supported platform functionality used by members and internal teams.
caseStudy.solution: Built and improved full-stack features using Next.js, Strapi, PostgreSQL, and external integrations. Worked on voting-related flows, admin/dashboard functionality, API communication, and business logic.
caseStudy.impact: Supported the delivery of a business-critical platform with complex workflows, member interaction, and internal management features.

items.title: Central Registry / One Stop Shop
items.description: Optimization of a web application for faster, more stable, and more accurate work with the central registry of live births and deceased persons.
items.impact: The work required data accuracy, reliable administrative flows, and careful handling of one of the country's most important state data sets.

whereHelp.title: Choose a challenge and see how my experience maps to real product and engineering problems.
whereHelp.intro: Select the type of problem you are trying to solve. Each view connects the challenge to practical delivery experience, relevant technologies, and real project context.

labels.ctaPrompt: Want to discuss a similar challenge?

items.label: Authentication & SSO
items.title: Secure login flows, SSO, and identity integration
items.description: I can help implement and improve authentication flows across frontend and backend applications. My experience includes Azure AD SSO, MSAL frontend integration, token exchange flows, environment-specific configuration, and QA/UAT validation.
items.impact: Helped connect secure enterprise authentication flows across multiple application layers.

items.label: Full-Stack Web Apps
items.title: From frontend UI to backend APIs
items.description: I build full-stack web applications with clean frontend structure, reliable backend APIs, and practical database design. I focus on building features that are maintainable, understandable, and ready for real business usage.
items.impact: Delivered business-facing features across both frontend and backend systems.

items.label: API Integrations
items.title: Connecting systems, services, and data
items.description: I work on integrations between frontend apps, backend services, third-party APIs, payment providers, CMS platforms, and internal business systems. I pay attention to error handling, data mapping, environment configuration, and edge cases.
items.impact: Helped connect multiple systems into smoother product workflows.

items.label: Admin Dashboards
items.title: Internal tools that make business operations easier
items.description: I can build admin dashboards and internal panels that help teams manage users, content, data, workflows, and business processes. I focus on clear UX, useful filtering, role-based behavior, and practical data presentation.
items.impact: Built and improved tools used by teams to manage real operational workflows.

items.label: Performance Improvements
items.title: Cleaner, faster, and more reliable applications
items.description: I can help identify slow parts of an application and improve frontend rendering, backend queries, API usage, and overall user experience. My experience includes working on performance improvements in insurance and business systems.
items.impact: Improved application behavior and reduced unnecessary friction in user workflows.

items.label: AI Features
items.title: Practical AI features that add real value
items.description: I can add AI-assisted features that support users instead of just looking flashy. Examples include role fit checking, content generation, smart recommendations, and guided product flows using affordable or free AI models.
items.impact: Added AI functionality focused on practical user value and simple UX.

items.label: Cloud & Deployments
items.title: Shipping features safely across environments
items.description: I have experience working with QA, UAT, and production deployment flows, environment variables, CI/CD processes, and coordination with different teams. I understand that delivery is not just code, but also configuration, testing, and release process.
items.impact: Supported delivery workflows across multiple environments and release stages.

items.label: Business Platforms
items.title: Software built around real business processes
items.description: I have worked on platforms for insurance, public-sector systems, voting/member areas, identity management, and enterprise business workflows. I focus on understanding the process behind the feature, not just implementing isolated tickets.
items.impact: Worked on systems where reliability, clarity, and business rules matter.

deliveryFlow.title: From Requirement to Release
deliveryFlow.intro: A visual breakdown of how I approach features, from the first requirement to tested delivery.

steps.label: Understand
steps.title: Understand the real requirement
steps.description: Before writing code, I try to understand the real problem behind the ticket or feature request. I look at the business goal, user flow, existing system behavior, dependencies, and possible edge cases.
focusPoints: What problem are we actually solving?
What systems or teams are involved?
Are there hidden dependencies or blockers?
steps.tools: Jira, Teams, Confluence, existing codebase, API contracts, product notes
steps.example: For enterprise SSO work, this meant understanding not just the login screen, but the full flow between Azure AD, frontend routing, backend token exchange, environment config, and QA/UAT validation.

steps.label: Break Down
steps.title: Break the work into clear technical pieces
steps.description: Once the requirement is clear, I split the work into frontend, backend, API, data, authentication, configuration, and deployment concerns. This helps reduce surprises during implementation and makes the delivery easier to coordinate.
steps.tools: Jira, technical notes, Git branches, API documentation, environment configs
steps.example: For SSO, the work was split into MSAL setup, frontend route handling, token exchange with backend API, environment-specific config, and deployment validation.

steps.label: Build
steps.title: Build clean, maintainable features
steps.description: During implementation, I focus on writing code that is clear, practical, and easy to maintain. I prefer simple architecture, reusable components, typed data structures, and predictable API communication.
steps.tools: React, Next.js, TypeScript, .NET, Angular, Strapi, SQL Server, PostgreSQL
steps.example: On full-stack platforms, this often means connecting UI flows with backend APIs, validating data, handling loading/error states, and keeping business logic understandable.

steps.label: Review
steps.title: Review, refine, and align with the team
steps.description: I treat code review as part of delivery, not just a formality. I use feedback to improve the implementation, align with team standards, and catch issues before they reach QA or production.
steps.tools: Bitbucket, GitHub, pull requests, Copilot review, team feedback, code comments
steps.example: For enterprise work, review often includes not only code quality, but also checking environment-specific behavior, authentication flow, and deployment readiness.

steps.label: Test
steps.title: Validate the feature across real flows
steps.description: I test the feature through realistic user flows, not only isolated happy paths. I check that the feature works with real configuration, real API responses, and expected edge cases.
steps.tools: QA, UAT, browser dev tools, Postman-like API checks, logs, test users
steps.example: For SSO work, testing included checking login redirects, token handling, protected routes, backend API exchange, and environment-specific behavior on QA/UAT.

steps.label: Release
steps.title: Support delivery across environments
steps.description: I understand that shipping is not only merging code. Release also includes configuration, environment variables, deployment pipelines, approvals, coordination, and final validation.
steps.tools: Harness, Jira, Bitbucket, GitHub, Azure, AWS, IIS, environment variables
steps.example: In enterprise delivery, I worked with QA/UAT deployments, Harness pipeline checks, environment variables, and coordination with different people involved in the release process.

steps.label: Improve
steps.title: Improve after feedback and real usage
steps.description: After delivery, I look for feedback, small improvements, performance issues, unclear UX, and technical cleanup opportunities. Good delivery does not stop when the feature is merged.
steps.tools: Jira feedback, QA comments, team messages, logs, browser testing, performance checks
steps.example: In business and insurance systems, this often meant improving existing flows, reducing friction, optimizing behavior, and making features easier for users and teams to work with.

roleFit.title: Paste a job description and see how the profile maps to the role.
roleFit.intro: This uses AI to compare the pasted role requirements against the actual experience, skills, and projects shown on this site. It does not invent missing experience.
roleFit.textareaPlaceholder: Paste the full job description here. Include responsibilities, requirements, stack, and any domain details you want checked.
roleFit.validationTooShort: Job description is too short. Please add more detail.
roleFit.validationTooLong: Job description is too long. Please shorten it.
roleFit.sampleJobDescription: We are hiring a Full Stack Developer with strong C#/.NET, ASP.NET Core, React, TypeScript, SQL Server, Azure, REST APIs, authentication, SSO, and cloud deployment experience. The role involves building enterprise web applications, integrating third-party systems, improving performance, working in Agile teams, and collaborating with product owners, designers, and QA engineers.
roleFit.emptyBody: Paste a real job description to get a recruiter-style summary, match score, strengths, potential gaps, and relevant technologies.

stack.title: Technologies I use to build enterprise applications and scalable systems.
stack.intro: My strongest delivery work sits around the Microsoft stack, modern frontend frameworks, cloud infrastructure, and integration-heavy business platforms.

contact.title: Need an enterprise app, a backend rebuild, or modernization support?
contact.body: I work with teams building business platforms, upgrading legacy systems, and shipping cloud-connected products. If you need senior .NET and full-stack delivery with clear ownership, get in touch.
contact.closing: Clear scope, senior engineering ownership, and systems built to stay maintainable.
